### Post by Hevydani on 2011-08-11
Hi I've recently changed my TV (from a panasonic, but the xorg still says it's that tv??)and now the highest available output is 800x600. I have an nvidia gts450 card with the latest drivers installed, yet still can't go above said resolution.

I'm not too up on editing the xorg.conf so was hoping someone may be able to point me in the right direction.

Here is my xorg config...


# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

### Post by realzippy on 2011-08-11
let the nvidia-driver create a new xorg.conf with your new TV plugged:

```
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```

restart Xserver or reboot.

If still resolution missed,post the new file and your monitor model.

---

### Post by Hevydani on 2011-08-11
Tried that but didn't work :/ now it's 640x480 lol

It's actually a Wharfdale TV. It was working the other day but I borrowed my Dad's Panasonic for a few days due to this TV being rubbish, as soon as I switched it back I've had troubles with the xorg. Here's the new xorg:



# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 270.41.06  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-07.nvidia.com)  Mon Apr 18 15:15:12 PDT 2011

### Post by realzippy on 2011-08-11
*which* wharfdale?exact model...

You have to edit
[I]HorizSync 28.0 - [COLOR="Red"]33.0[/COLOR]
VertRefresh 43.0 - 72.0
[/I]
to the specific values for your wharfdale...

---

### Post by Hevydani on 2011-08-11
lcd2610af

---

### Post by realzippy on 2011-08-11
Seems to be not so easy to find a manual for wharfdale lcd2610af online.
You have a manual?Needed Hsync and Vrefresh values should be there.

Btw,you mentioned the wharfdale used to work once if I  understand correctly..
So that xorg.conf file could still exist,named as
xorg.conf.old or xorg.conf.backup or something...check
your folder:
/etc/X11

Continue searching for values meanwhile..

---

### Post by realzippy on 2011-08-11
Well,it has a native resolution 1280 x 768 pixels.
So you could use

28-51 H
50-72 V

---

### Post by Hevydani on 2011-08-11
